twitter_thread: 1 in 5 junior dev positions are now being filled by AI systems, not humans
GitHub Copilot and Tabnine boost coding speed by 25%, but 60% of users can't explain the code
Over-reliance on AI tools leads to 30% more bugs and errors in shipped software
Using Codex to practice explaining AI-generated code can reduce errors by 20%
Focusing on design and architecture can increase developer value by 40%
How are you adapting your skills to stay employable as a software developer in an AI-driven world? #softwaredevelopment #ai
Are coding bootcamps preparing students for an AI-augmented workforce?
pack.linkedin: 40% of young developers are struggling to find employment due to AI. Meanwhile, AI-assisted coding tools like GitHub Copilot and Tabnine are increasing coding speed by 25%, but also leading to 30% more bugs and errors. To stay relevant, developers must focus on high-level design and architecture, and practice explaining AI-generated code using tools like Codex. What strategies are you using to stay employable?
